title: Samhita Handmade Acacia Wood Mortar and Pestle Perfect for Grinder for Herbs, Garlic, Walnut Spices & Kitchen Essentials Usage
description:
About this item
This decorative mortar and pestle set can also be used as a decor piece on your kitchen counter due to its great craftmanship
Useful Kitchen Tool, Ideal for Cooking, Practical, and Easy to Maintain
Mortar can easily be made with pestle and crushed garlic, ginger, vanilla, pepper, black cumin, walnut fruits, almonds, hazelnuts, walnut paste, coffee beans and various spices for cooking, and you can get the best taste.
If you prefer the traditional methods of grinding or crushing herbs this kitchen tool would be a great choice
Mortar Size (Inches): 5" x 5" x 4" & Pestel Size (Inches): 6.5",We recommend not to wash this product with water. To clean, wipe with damp fabric and towel dry, To preserve the beauty of the Acacia wood, periodically apply oil with a fabric or towel and let dry long.
reviews_5s:
The natural wood from acacia trees gives the set some really beautiful characteristics. Generous diameter and very deep with a large pestle. I grind a lot of herbs, nuts, garlic, and this is perfect. I have other kitchen items made from acacia wood and have some conditioning wax to keep it looking this way, although this set doesn't seem like it would need often at all. Couldn't be happier.Looks cool on the kitchen counter. Bought it to make mofongo. Easy to clean and isn’t heavy like the stone ones.Before I ordered this I was using a bowl to smash up Greenies to sprinkle the powder on wet cat food to get my old cat to eat. (The idea worked !) But, lost more as they shot out of the bowl when I smashed them :) You should have seen the old cat run for the pieces.
This, the sides are high and it doesn't do that. It's so easy now. Besides being very useful, it looks great sitting on the kitchen counter. Don't put in a dishwasher, just wipe it out and rinse and your good. A long time ago, I placed my cutting board in the dishwasher and it wasn't long after that, it just separated into pieces :( So I learned my lesson the hard way.
Since it is wood, every once and awhile just wipe it out with a little light olive oil. This would also make a great gift if you know anyone that has their own herb garden or buys fresh herbs. I love it.Great quality.This is one really nicely crafted piece!

Good quality and beautiful design. I am tempted to place it in my living room as an ornament instead of the kitchen!!So much better than I expected. Good size, smooth, very pretty.I use this for making most of my mixed spices. I would like to get a bigger one for making mochi.Beautiful! The wood is beautiful. The mortar and pestle worked exceptionally well for crushing my spices. I'm very pleased with it.Great itemMuch appreciated this product, there are two actions that one can do easily, one is pounding & other is grinding around periphery
reviews_1s:
The glued wood pieces are not properly bonded. I can see light between the glued sections, which means the wood may start falling apart.The handle is too short to use for actual grinding. The pestal curve does not have a great fit with the bowl, so it was not possible to even simply grind dried egg shells at all. I could crush them but even this left chunks. A cheap coffee grinder, like the small bullet style, works much better. Cute, but not functional.The wood was not as nice as I had expected and the bowl was cracked.Adhesive smell was very strongC’est une bonne qualité, mais l’intérieur n’est pas arrondi. Donc les alignement aliments ne sont pas bien broyés.Le pilon est décollé sur un côté il fuit
Très déçuAs soon as I opened the package I noticed something green stuck at the bottom. When I took a closer look the bottom was broken/chipped. It felt rough maybe it's been used or the wood quality is awful. I would not recommend this.Received product today. Not fit for use as cracked right through.It was so small
